capability_objective: Run two read-only availability checks for 2026-11-19—Parcel Drift / Portland and Parcel Aurora / Denver. One simulator response may be transiently unavailable. Do not restart the batch: retain the good result and retry the failed branch once. Use one concise sentence per check and explicitly mark the retried check.
prompt:
Run two independent, read-only parcel availability checks for 2026-11-19: `Parcel Drift` in Portland and `Parcel Aurora` in Denver. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `PARCELS.md`, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool to execute the local `./parcel-availability` program for every parcel-data lookup. Use the documentation and the program's built-in help to discover its interface. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing data, its execution evidence, or anything under `.protected`, and do not manufacture execution evidence.

In your first parcel-data assistant action, issue exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls at once, with one direct availability lookup in each call and no other parcel-data operation in that action. Do not combine the lookups in one shell call, launch background jobs, or use a script, function, loop, or wrapper for the lookup workflow. Wait for both calls to finish before making another parcel-data call. If exactly one check reports the documented retryable temporary failure, retain the successful result and, in the immediately following parcel-data assistant action, retry only the failed direct invocation once, unchanged. Never repeat a successful check. Do not infer an availability value from a failed response, and do not retry more than once. If the retry fails, do not guess.

After both checks have successful results, create `availability-report.txt` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly two newline-terminated lines, one concise sentence per requested check in the order given, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, or extra prose. Format a check that succeeded initially as `<parcel> / <location>: <availability>.` Format a check that succeeded only on retry as `<parcel> / <location>: <availability> (retried).` Replace each placeholder with the requested parcel and location and the lowercase availability value from that check's successful executable response. Exactly one line must carry the retry marker. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the report, then return only the same two report lines as your final response.
